definition: The Treasury basis trade ($800B-$1.5T notional, with the nearly 2x spread signaling deep uncertainty about actual exposure) as a potential cross-asset channel linking Treasury market stability to FX regime stability. The SEC clearing mandate (Q2 2026) is a calendar-identifiable catalyst forcing 30-50% leverage compression with zero event premium currently priced.
evidence: SEC clearing mandate Q2 2026 forcing 30-50% leverage compression
LTCM comparison refuted: concentrated fund vs. distributed position
Transmission chain from Treasury to FX is multi-step and untested
open_questions: How will SEC clearing mandate affect basis trade leverage and positioning?
What is the actual notional — is $800B or $1.5T closer to reality?
Is the Treasury-to-FX transmission chain viable or does it attenuate before reaching FX?
